response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with strong breadth across web, backend, and embedded systems, plus genuine geospatial experience via GISCollective and Green Map. However, the role specifically requires modern C++ (or Rust) for routing tile pipelines, and the resume shows only brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments a decade ago. Primary languages are TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — no demonstrated Rust experience and no navigation/routing pipeline work. Worth a screening call given the geospatial background and low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic, but technical fit on the core stack is weak.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS experience building and operating a map data platform (GISCollective, Green Map)
Low-latency, high-throughput backend experience at Sociomantic (millions of req/s, async I/O, fibers)
Strong engineering discipline — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testability focus
concerns: No recent modern C++ experience; last C/C++ role was 2014–2015 at National Instruments
No Rust experience listed anywhere in the resume or projects
No prior work on routing, navigation guidance, or tile pipeline systems specifically
